The genus Ouratea is further classified under the family Ochidaceae, which comprises various flowering plants. Ouratea congesta is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This classification places Ouratea congesta within the larger plant kingdom, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta.

intro: Ouratea congesta, also known as the 'Congested Ouratea', is a plant species in the family Ochnaceae, native to tropical regions. Traditionally, Ouratea congesta has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and infections, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ouratea congesta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Ouratea congesta is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
